title: raoulia bryoides
intro: Raoulia bryoides, also known as the 'New Zealand astel', is a succulent plant species native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand. This plant has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, and has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antibacterial activities. The health benefits of Raoulia bryoides include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and potentially combat infections. Herbal preparations of Raoulia bryoides can be made by infusing the leaves in water or oil, and it is often used in topical applications or as a natural remedy for skin issues.
